title: Styx Interchange: Localizing Refusal Behavior in LLMs
description: Rapid mechanistic interpretability sprint on Qwen3-0.6B comparing activation patching against input gradients to isolate where causal refusal behavior concentrates across layers.

title: David vs. Goliath: Verifiable Agent-to-Agent Jailbreaking via Reinforcement Learning
description: Led research formalizing Tag-Along Attacks, where adversaries exploit tool necessity in LLM agents. The Slingshot RL framework finds simple imperative jailbreaks with 67% success and transfers zero-shot to closed models including Gemini.

title: ARENA 5.0 Capstone: Internal Representations in SONAR Autoencoders
description: Mechanistic interpretability sprint probing whether SONAR text autoencoders encode correctness across code, grammar, arithmetic, and chess domains.

title: AI Safety Camp 2025: Reasoning Capabilities of LLMs
description: International mechanistic interpretability project studying reasoning capabilities in language models; the resulting paper was published on arXiv and accepted into Findings of EACL 2026.

title: Open-source AI safety tooling
description: Contributed to evaluation and environment tooling, including a merged UK AISI inspect_ai fix for vLLM startup behavior and a Prime Intellect Environments text-retrieval environment.

title: How do mice vary in the amount of past information they use to make decisions under uncertainty?
description: Neuromatch Academy Computational Neuroscience course project on decision-making under uncertainty.
